page: UpCloud PREMIUM-48xCPU-256GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 48 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with x86_64 architecture. The CPU configuration provides 48 physical cores with a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz and a shared allocation model. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of memory, yielding approximately 5.33 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with a 500 GB local SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core CPU execution, with stress-ng single-core metrics placing in the top 10%. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores rank in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong, though large-block uncached read and write speeds fall into the average tier. Cryptographic and vector execution capabilities are supported by hardware flags such as AVX-512 and AES. Database operations, Redis SET performance, and static web serving throughput all register in the top 25%, demonstrating robust capabilities for high-throughput workloads.

This server configuration is suitable for multi-threaded processing, database hosting, and web serving. The high core count and strong multi-core decompression performance support parallelized data processing and compression tasks. However, the shared CPU allocation model represents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, isolated CPU performance. Large-scale LLM inference is constrained, as shown by weak prompt processing speeds on 7B models, making it less suitable for complex AI inference tasks.
description: UpCloud PREMIUM-48xCPU-256GB is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 48 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 256.0 GB of RAM, a 500 GB local SSD, and one public IPv4 address. Operating with a shared CPU allocation, the server delivers top-tier single-core performance and strong multi-core capabilities, as evidenced by top 10% stress-ng single-core and top 25% PassMark CPU benchmarks. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong, while large-block uncached operations remain average. The hardware supports advanced vector and cryptographic instruction sets. This configuration is well-suited for high-throughput database operations, multi-threaded data processing, and static web serving, though the shared CPU allocation and lack of GPU acceleration present tradeoffs for sustained compute-intensive or large-scale AI inference workloads.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-48xCPU-256GB is a KVM virtual server with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 500 GB local SSD, delivering top-tier single-core performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-48xCPU-256GB features 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 500 GB SSD, optimized for databases and multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-core virtual server featuring AMD EPYC processors and local SSD storage, optimized for databases and parallel data processing.
bullet_points: 48 AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs with shared CPU allocation
256 GB system memory and 500 GB local SSD storage
Top-tier single-core and strong multi-core benchmark performance
Advanced vector (AVX-512) and cryptographic instruction support
Optimized for database operations, Redis, and static web serving
